mapper.sql: id, patient_id, scheduling_id, case_number, real_name, gender, card_number, birthdate, age, age_type, home_address, visit_date, noon, deptment_id, employee_id, regist_level_id, settle_category_id, is_book, regist_method, regist_money, visit_state, check_in_time
mapper.select: SELECT r.id, r.patient_id, r.scheduling_id, r.case_number, r.real_name, r.gender, r.card_number, r.birthdate, r.age, r.age_type, r.home_address, r.visit_date, r.noon, r.deptment_id, r.employee_id, r.regist_level_id, r.settle_category_id, r.is_book, r.regist_method, r.regist_money, r.visit_state, r.check_in_time, upm.relation AS relation FROM register r INNER JOIN user_patient_managed upm ON upm.patient_id = r.patient_id WHERE upm.user_id = #{userId} AND r.visit_state != 4 ORDER BY r.visit_date DESC NULLS LAST, r.id DESC
SELECT FROM register WHERE visit_state = 1 AND check_in_time IS NULL ORDER BY visit_date ASC
mapper.update: UPDATE register SET visit_state = 7 WHERE visit_state = 1 AND visit_date < #{deadline} AND check_in_time IS NULL
UPDATE register SET visit_state = 7 WHERE id = #{id} AND visit_state = 1 AND check_in_time IS NULL
mapper.select: SELECT last_value + CASE WHEN is_called THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM register_id_seq
SELECT setval( pg_get_serial_sequence('register', 'id'), COALESCE((SELECT MAX(id) FROM register), 1), true )
mapper.update: UPDATE register SET patient_id = #{patientId}, scheduling_id = #{schedulingId}, real_name = #{realName}, gender = #{gender}, card_number = #{cardNumber}, birthdate = #{birthdate}, age = #{age}, age_type = #{ageType}, home_address = #{homeAddress}, visit_date = #{visitDate}, noon = #{noon}, deptment_id = #{deptmentId}, employee_id = #{employeeId}, regist_level_id = #{registLevelId}, settle_category_id = #{settleCategoryId}, is_book = #{isBook}, regist_method = #{registMethod}, regist_money = #{registMoney}, visit_state = #{visitState} WHERE id = #{id}
UPDATE register SET visit_state = #{visitState} WHERE id = #{id}
UPDATE register SET visit_state = #{visitState} WHERE id = #{id}
UPDATE register SET check_in_time = #{checkInTime} WHERE id = #{id}
mapper.select: WITH cur AS ( SELECT employee_id, DATE(visit_date) AS d, check_in_time FROM register WHERE id = #{id} ) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM register r, cur WHERE r.id <> #{id} AND r.visit_state = 1 AND r.check_in_time IS NOT NULL AND r.employee_id = cur.employee_id AND DATE(r.visit_date) = cur.d AND r.check_in_time < cur.check_in_time
mapper.delete: UPDATE register SET visit_state = 4 WHERE id = #{id}
mapper.select: SELECT case_number FROM register WHERE patient_id = #{patientId} ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1
